Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. - Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.

Looking at what's actually possible: the Agent tool supports a `model` parameter that accepts "sonnet" (which maps to Claude Sonnet 5), so I can spawn subagents with that model directly on each call. For a persistent setup across sessions, they could define custom agents in `.claude/agents/*.md` with `model: sonnet` in the frontmatter, or I can remember their preference. No special command is needed — I can simply pass the sonnet model parameter whenever delegating tasks.

Since the user explicitly asked if I can help set this up, I should create a custom agent definition file and save a memory of this preference to make it durable. I should also check if a `.claude/agents` directory already exists in the project before creating the definition. I'll spawn the claude-code-guide agent to confirm the exact frontmatter syntax for custom agents and how the model parameter maps to specific Claude versions, then check the project's .claude directory and create a durable agent definition with the right configuration. I need to gather exact details on how Claude Code CLI handles custom agent definitions and model overrides — specifically the frontmatter schema, field names, accepted model values, and precedence rules when spawning subagents. I'll run this query synchronously since I need the answer before writing the agent file itself. Then I'll create the worker agent definition with the appropriate configuration.

After that, I need to set up the memory file for tracking this orchestrator setup and update the project's MEMORY.md accordingly — reading it first to check if it exists, then either creating it fresh or appending the reference. I'm planning to batch the initial file system checks (globbing the .claude directories and reading MEMORY.md) in parallel with the agent query, though I need to be careful about whether running the guide agent synchronously will block the other operations. I can pass `model: "sonnet"` directly in each Agent call, which is the simplest approach. I should also mention the Workflow tool as an option if they want to orchestrate larger fan-outs across multiple sonnet agents — they'd use the `ultracode` keyword to enable it, and agent calls within workflows also accept the model parameter.

content.text:
## Summary: Subagent model configuration in Claude Code (verified against current docs, last updated 2026-07-06)

Sources: [sub-agents](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents.md), [model-config](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config.md), [agents](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agents.md), [agent-view](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-view.md)

### 1. `.claude/agents/*.md` frontmatter schema

Only `name` and `description` are required. Full field list:

| Field | Accepts |
|---|---|
| `name` | lowercase letters/hyphens, unique within scope; the value hooks see as `agent_type` |
| `description` | text — when Claude should delegate to this subagent |
| `tools` | allowlist (e.g. `Read, Grep, Glob`); supports `Agent(type1, type2)` to restrict which subagents it can spawn |
| `disallowedTools` | denylist, applied before `tools` |
| `model` | **`sonnet`, `opus`, `haiku`, `fable`** (aliases) · a **full model ID** like `claude-sonnet-5` or `claude-opus-4-8` (same values `--model` accepts) · **`inherit`**. Omitted = defaults to `inherit` |
| `permissionMode` | `default`, `acceptEdits`, `auto`, `dontAsk`, `bypassPermissions`, `plan`, or `manual` (alias for `default`, v2.1.200+) |
| `maxTurns`, `skills`, `mcpServers`, `hooks`, `memory` (`user`/`project`/`local`), `background`, `effort`, `isolation` (`worktree`), `color`, `initialPrompt` | see docs for details |

**Does `model: sonnet` resolve to Sonnet 5?** Yes, currently, on the Anthropic API: *"opus resolves to Opus 4.8 and sonnet resolves to Sonnet 5"* (requires Claude Code **v2.1.197+**). On Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry it's still Sonnet 4.5; on Claude Platform on AWS, Sonnet 4.6. **This is exactly the kind of thing that's version- and provider-dependent** — aliases "update over time," so pin a full ID if you need certainty.

**Scope precedence** (name collision → higher wins):
1. Managed/org settings `.claude/agents/` (highest)
2. `--agents` CLI flag (session-only, not persisted)
3. `.claude/agents/` — **project**, discovered walking up from cwd to repo root
4. `~/.claude/agents/` — **user**, all projects
5. Plugin's `agents/` directory (lowest)

Your project currently has no `.claude/agents/*.md` files, so this would be a first-time setup here.

### 2. `/agents` command — version-dependent, and easy to confuse with `claude agents`

- **As of v2.1.198** (current): `/agents` **no longer opens a wizard**. It just prints a notice pointing to the `.claude/agents/` / `~/.claude/agents/` file locations — creation/editing is done by asking Claude or hand-editing the files.
- **v2.1.197 and earlier**: `/agents` opened an interactive terminal wizard with a **Running** tab (live subagents) and a **Library** tab (create/edit/delete).
- **Don't confuse it with `claude agents`** (no slash, run from the shell) — that opens *Agent View*, an unrelated feature for dispatching/monitoring background sessions, not subagent definitions.

Since I can't run `claude --version` myself, confirm your version if this matters.

### 3. Per-call model override vs. frontmatter — override wins

When Claude invokes a subagent via the Agent/Task tool, it can pass a `model` parameter for that specific call. Full resolution order:

1. **`CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL`** env var (if set to anything but `inherit`) — wins over everything
2. **Per-invocation `model` parameter**
3. **Subagent definition's `model` frontmatter**
4. **Main conversation's model**

So: **the per-call override beats the frontmatter's `model`**, but an org's `availableModels` allowlist is checked at every level and silently falls back to the inherited model if a value is excluded. Also as of v2.1.198, subagents inherit the main conversation's extended-thinking on/off state — there's no separate per-subagent thinking toggle.

### 4. Yes — `CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL` forces one model onto every subagent

> *"The model to use for all subagents and agent teams. Overrides the per-invocation `model` parameter and the subagent definition's `model` frontmatter. Set to `inherit` to use normal model resolution instead."*

There's no plain top-level settings.json key for this (like `"subagentModel"`) — it's exposed only as an environment variable, so you'd set it inside the `env` block, same as your other `ANTHROPIC_*` vars.

---

### Applied to your settings.json

You already have `ANTHROPIC_MODEL: "claude-fable-5"` (main conversation = Fable 5) and `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL: "claude-sonnet-5"` (also `OPUS`/`HAIKU` pinned to the same ID). Two things worth flagging:

- **`CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL` is not set today**, so any subagent that omits `model:` in its frontmatter (or is a built-in like Explore/Plan/general-purpose, which default to `inherit`) will run on **Fable 5**, not Sonnet 5 — inherit means "same as main conversation," not "Sonnet."
- To get Sonnet 5 on subagents while keeping Fable 5 on the main thread, you have two options:
  - **Global, unoverridable**: add `"CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL": "claude-sonnet-5"` to your `env` block — forces *every* subagent onto Sonnet 5, ignoring any per-agent `model:` frontmatter.
  - **Per-agent**: put `model: claude-sonnet-5` (or `model: sonnet`, since your `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` is already pinned there) in each `.claude/agents/*.md` file's frontmatter, leaving other subagents free to inherit Fable 5.

One more caveat: your `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` (`https://nexaxis.ai`) is a non-first-party/custom gateway, not `api.anthropic.com`. Docs note that on a custom base URL, Claude Code **skips client-side validation of model-ID strings** and passes them straight through — the provider-specific alias defaults quoted above (e.g., "sonnet → Sonnet 5") formally apply to the Anthropic API, Bedrock, Vertex, Foundry, and Claude Platform on AWS specifically. toolUseResult.prompt:
I need exact, verified details about Claude Code CLI (as of mid-2026) on making subagents run on a specific model (Sonnet 5, model ID claude-sonnet-5) while the main conversation runs a different model (Fable 5). Please answer:

1. Custom agent definitions in .claude/agents/*.md — the exact frontmatter schema: which fields are supported (name, description, tools, model, etc.)? What values does the `model` field accept — short aliases like `sonnet`, full model IDs like `claude-sonnet-5`, or both? Does `model: sonnet` currently resolve to Sonnet 5? Difference/precedence between project-level (.claude/agents/) and user-level (~/.claude/agents/)?
2. Is there an interactive slash command to create/manage subagents (e.g. /agents)? What does it do?
3. When the main agent spawns a subagent via the Task/Agent tool, can it override the model per call, and which wins: the per-call override or the agent definition's frontmatter model?
4. Is there any settings.json or environment-variable way to set a default model for all subagents?

Return a concise factual summary with exact field names and accepted values, noting anything version-dependent or uncertain.

content.text:
## Summary: Subagent model configuration in Claude Code (verified against current docs, last updated 2026-07-06)

Sources: [sub-agents](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents.md), [model-config](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config.md), [agents](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agents.md), [agent-view](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-view.md)

### 1. `.claude/agents/*.md` frontmatter schema

Only `name` and `description` are required. Full field list:

| Field | Accepts |
|---|---|
| `name` | lowercase letters/hyphens, unique within scope; the value hooks see as `agent_type` |
| `description` | text — when Claude should delegate to this subagent |
| `tools` | allowlist (e.g. `Read, Grep, Glob`); supports `Agent(type1, type2)` to restrict which subagents it can spawn |
| `disallowedTools` | denylist, applied before `tools` |
| `model` | **`sonnet`, `opus`, `haiku`, `fable`** (aliases) · a **full model ID** like `claude-sonnet-5` or `claude-opus-4-8` (same values `--model` accepts) · **`inherit`**. Omitted = defaults to `inherit` |
| `permissionMode` | `default`, `acceptEdits`, `auto`, `dontAsk`, `bypassPermissions`, `plan`, or `manual` (alias for `default`, v2.1.200+) |
| `maxTurns`, `skills`, `mcpServers`, `hooks`, `memory` (`user`/`project`/`local`), `background`, `effort`, `isolation` (`worktree`), `color`, `initialPrompt` | see docs for details |

**Does `model: sonnet` resolve to Sonnet 5?** Yes, currently, on the Anthropic API: *"opus resolves to Opus 4.8 and sonnet resolves to Sonnet 5"* (requires Claude Code **v2.1.197+**). On Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry it's still Sonnet 4.5; on Claude Platform on AWS, Sonnet 4.6. **This is exactly the kind of thing that's version- and provider-dependent** — aliases "update over time," so pin a full ID if you need certainty.

**Scope precedence** (name collision → higher wins):
1. Managed/org settings `.claude/agents/` (highest)
2. `--agents` CLI flag (session-only, not persisted)
3. `.claude/agents/` — **project**, discovered walking up from cwd to repo root
4. `~/.claude/agents/` — **user**, all projects
5. Plugin's `agents/` directory (lowest)

Your project currently has no `.claude/agents/*.md` files, so this would be a first-time setup here.

### 2. `/agents` command — version-dependent, and easy to confuse with `claude agents`

- **As of v2.1.198** (current): `/agents` **no longer opens a wizard**. It just prints a notice pointing to the `.claude/agents/` / `~/.claude/agents/` file locations — creation/editing is done by asking Claude or hand-editing the files.
- **v2.1.197 and earlier**: `/agents` opened an interactive terminal wizard with a **Running** tab (live subagents) and a **Library** tab (create/edit/delete).
- **Don't confuse it with `claude agents`** (no slash, run from the shell) — that opens *Agent View*, an unrelated feature for dispatching/monitoring background sessions, not subagent definitions.

Since I can't run `claude --version` myself, confirm your version if this matters.

### 3. Per-call model override vs. frontmatter — override wins

When Claude invokes a subagent via the Agent/Task tool, it can pass a `model` parameter for that specific call. Full resolution order:

1. **`CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL`** env var (if set to anything but `inherit`) — wins over everything
2. **Per-invocation `model` parameter**
3. **Subagent definition's `model` frontmatter**
4. **Main conversation's model**

So: **the per-call override beats the frontmatter's `model`**, but an org's `availableModels` allowlist is checked at every level and silently falls back to the inherited model if a value is excluded. Also as of v2.1.198, subagents inherit the main conversation's extended-thinking on/off state — there's no separate per-subagent thinking toggle.

### 4. Yes — `CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL` forces one model onto every subagent

> *"The model to use for all subagents and agent teams. Overrides the per-invocation `model` parameter and the subagent definition's `model` frontmatter. Set to `inherit` to use normal model resolution instead."*

There's no plain top-level settings.json key for this (like `"subagentModel"`) — it's exposed only as an environment variable, so you'd set it inside the `env` block, same as your other `ANTHROPIC_*` vars.

---

### Applied to your settings.json

You already have `ANTHROPIC_MODEL: "claude-fable-5"` (main conversation = Fable 5) and `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL: "claude-sonnet-5"` (also `OPUS`/`HAIKU` pinned to the same ID). Two things worth flagging:

- **`CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL` is not set today**, so any subagent that omits `model:` in its frontmatter (or is a built-in like Explore/Plan/general-purpose, which default to `inherit`) will run on **Fable 5**, not Sonnet 5 — inherit means "same as main conversation," not "Sonnet."
- To get Sonnet 5 on subagents while keeping Fable 5 on the main thread, you have two options:
  - **Global, unoverridable**: add `"CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL": "claude-sonnet-5"` to your `env` block — forces *every* subagent onto Sonnet 5, ignoring any per-agent `model:` frontmatter.
  - **Per-agent**: put `model: claude-sonnet-5` (or `model: sonnet`, since your `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` is already pinned there) in each `.claude/agents/*.md` file's frontmatter, leaving other subagents free to inherit Fable 5.

One more caveat: your `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` (`https://nexaxis.ai`) is a non-first-party/custom gateway, not `api.anthropic.com`. Docs note that on a custom base URL, Claude Code **skips client-side validation of model-ID strings** and passes them straight through — the provider-specific alias defaults quoted above (e.g., "sonnet → Sonnet 5") formally apply to the Anthropic API, Bedrock, Vertex, Foundry, and Claude Platform on AWS specifically. Since you've explicitly pinned every alias via `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_*_MODEL`, you've already followed the docs' recommended practice for gateway deployments ("pin model versions... before rolling out"), so this ambiguity shouldn't bite you — just know that any resolution error would surface from nexaxis.ai's response rather than a Claude Code preflight check. This also matches why `ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH: "true"` is present in your config: it's required to keep MCP tool search working, since it's disabled by default on any non-first-party `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`.

If you want me to actually draft the `.claude/agents/*.md` file(s), or wire up `CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL` in settings.json, I (or `/update-config`) can do that directly — just say which approach you want.

完成了。总结如下：

**我是谁**：我是 Claude Code（Anthropic 官方 CLI 编程助手），当前主对话由 **Fable 5**（`claude-fable-5`）驱动——Claude 5 家族里高于 Opus 的 Mythos 级模型。

两个注意点：

- 这个环境变量是"一刀切"的：以后如果某个子任务你想让它也用 Fable 5 跑，需要先把这个变量删掉或改成 `"inherit"`，告诉我一声即可，我可以帮你改。
- 你走的是 nexaxis.ai 网关，模型 ID 会原样透传。你已经把各档位模型都固定到了 `claude-sonnet-5`，所以不会有别名解析歧义的问题。

✅ 测试通过，模式已完全生效。验证了三层：

1. **环境变量已加载**：本次会话里 `CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=claude-sonnet-5`，`ANTHROPIC_MODEL=claude-fable-5`，和预期一致。
2. **实际派发验证**：我刚才真实派发了一个子 agent（没有传任何 model 参数，就用默认方式），它汇报自己跑的是 **Sonnet 5，模型 ID `claude-sonnet-5`** ——说明强制覆盖起作用了。
3. **主会话确认**：我自己仍然跑在 **Fable 5**（`claude-fable-5`）上。

也就是说现在的分工正是你要的：我（Fable 5）负责理解需求、拆任务、把关汇总；所有派出去干活的子 agent 自动跑 Sonnet 5，不需要你或我做任何额外指定。

顺带一提，子 agent 那次调用只花了约 1.7 万 token、7 秒，开销很轻。现在可以直接丢真实任务给我了——我会按"拆解 → 并行派发 Sonnet worker → 汇总验证"的流程来处理。

input.description: 安装 taste-skill 技能
input.prompt:
Task: Install the Claude Code skill from https://github.com/Leonxlnx/taste-skill onto this Windows machine (shell is Git Bash, user home is C:\Users\Jony.li).

Steps:
1. Inspect the repo first: use WebFetch on https://github.com/Leonxlnx/taste-skill (and its README) or `git clone https://github.com/Leonxlnx/taste-skill` into a temp directory, then examine its structure. A Claude Code skill is a directory containing a SKILL.md (plus optional references/, scripts/, assets/). The repo may be a single skill at its root, or contain one or more skill subfolders.
2. Check where this machine's existing user-level skills live: list C:\Users\Jony.li\.claude\skills\ (there should be existing skills like o1key-imagegen). Mirror that location.
3. Install: if the repo README specifies an explicit install method, follow it. Otherwise copy the skill folder(s) into C:\Users\Jony.li\.claude\skills\<skill-name>\ WITHOUT any .git directory. If the repo root itself is the skill (SKILL.md at root), install it as folder name matching the `name:` field in SKILL.md frontmatter (fallback: taste-skill).
4. Verify: confirm the installed folder contains SKILL.md and print its frontmatter.

Return in plain text, concise:
- What the repo/skill actually is and does (from its README/SKILL.md)
- The exact absolute path where you installed it
- The skill's `name:` and `description:` from SKILL.md frontmatter
- Any extra setup the README requires (dependencies, env vars, API keys)
- Anything that failed or looked suspicious (e.g. if the repo is NOT actually a Claude Code skill, say so clearly and do not guess-install; describe what it is instead)

input.description: 部署项目启动依赖
input.prompt:
Task: Install and verify the startup dependencies for the Next.js project at C:\Users\Jony.li\Desktop\o1key-vision-workbench (Windows, Git Bash shell).

Context: it is a Next.js 15 + React 19 + Tailwind 4 app named "taste-studio", npm-based (package-lock.json and .npmrc exist in the root). node_modules does not exist yet. A local proxy is auto-configured by the shell profile, so network access should work.

Steps:
1. Read README.md and HANDOFF.md in the project root for documented setup/startup requirements (env vars, API keys, config files, external services). Also glance at .npmrc to see if a custom registry is configured.
2. Check installed node and npm versions (`node -v`, `npm -v`). Next.js 15 needs Node >= 18.18.
3. Run `npm install` in the project root. Capture how many packages were added and any errors or notable warnings.
4. Smoke-test startup: start `npm run dev` as a background task, then poll http://localhost:3000 with curl for up to ~90 seconds until you get an HTTP response (first compile can be slow). Record the HTTP status.
5. Then shut the dev server down cleanly. IMPORTANT on Windows: do NOT run `taskkill //IM node.exe` (that would kill unrelated Node processes including the Claude Code host). Instead find the specific PID listening on port 3000 via `netstat -ano | grep :3000` and kill only that PID with `taskkill //F //PID <pid>`, or kill the background shell you started. Verify port 3000 is free afterwards.
6. If install or boot fails, capture the exact error output.

Return in plain text, concise:
- node/npm versions found
- npm install result (package count, errors, warnings worth knowing; ignore routine deprecation noise)
- Smoke test result: did the dev server boot, what HTTP status did localhost:3000 return, and confirmation you shut it down
- The exact command the user should run to start the app
- Any configuration the user still must provide before the app is fully functional, according to README.md / HANDOFF.md (e.g. API keys entered in a settings panel)

content.text:
Installation and smoke test complete. Summary:

**node/npm versions**
- node v24.12.0, npm 11.6.2 — both comfortably satisfy the Next.js 15 requirement (Node ≥ 18.18).

**npm install**
- Clean: `added 54 packages in 20s`, zero errors, zero warnings (not even deprecation noise). `.npmrc` uses the default npm registry with `legacy-peer-deps=true`, `fund=false`, `audit=false` — nothing custom to worry about.

**Smoke test**
- `npm run dev` booted fine: Next.js 15.5.20 ready in 3.3s, first compile of `/` took 14.2s (10386 modules). Polling curl got **HTTP 200** on the 3rd attempt (~15s after launch). Server log showed `GET / 200` repeatedly, no runtime errors.
- Two harmless warnings appeared in the dev log, worth knowing but not blocking:
  - Next.js "detected multiple lockfiles" and inferred the workspace root as `C:\Users\Jony.li\pnpm-lock.yaml` instead of the project folder (because a `pnpm-lock.yaml` exists in the home dir and another `package-lock.json` sits in `C:\Users\Jony.li\Desktop\`). Purely cosmetic; can be silenced by setting `outputFileTracingRoot` in `next.config`, but doesn't affect functionality.
  - Repeated `Watchpack Error (initial scan)` lines for `C:\DumpStack.log.tmp`, `hiberfil.sys`, `pagefile.sys`, `swapfile.sys` — the file watcher trying (and failing) to stat protected Windows system files at the drive root. Harmless, common on Windows, safe to ignore.
- Shutdown: identified PID 34500 as the sole listener on port 3000 via `netstat -ano | grep :3000`, killed it with `taskkill //F //PID 34500` (succeeded), and confirmed port 3000 has no LISTENING socket afterward (only a stale client-side TIME_WAIT entry remained, which clears on its own). No other node.exe processes were touched.

**Command to start the app**
```
npm run dev
```
then open http://localhost:3000. (Production alternative: `npm run build && npm run start`.)

**Configuration still required before the app is fully functional**
Per README.md/HANDOFF.md: on first visit to localhost:3000, a Settings panel (⚙ top-right) auto-opens — the user must enter their **o1key API Bearer token** (created in the o1key backend) there before generation works. It's saved only to local `data/settings.json` (gitignored) and never leaves the machine except to the chosen o1key endpoint. Default route is "全球加速 (api.o1key.cn)"; if that fails, switch to "CF加速" or "美国直连" in the same settings panel, and optionally use "测试连接" to verify reachability. Per HANDOFF.md, connectivity has already been verified end-to-end with a fake token (got the expected 401 "令牌被拒绝"), but actual paid image generation has not yet been exercised with a real token — that's the next step once a real token is supplied.

content.text:
Installation complete. Summary:

**What the repo is:** `github.com/Leonxlnx/taste-skill` is not a single skill — it's a legitimate Claude Code **plugin/marketplace repo** (has valid `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` and `marketplace.json`) bundling **13 separate skills** under a `skills/` subfolder. All are frontend-design "anti-slop" prompt skills for AI coding agents (Claude Code / Codex / Cursor): things like `taste-skill` (flagship default), `gpt-tasteskill`, `redesign-skill`, `soft-skill`, `brutalist-skill`, `minimalist-skill`, `stitch-skill`, `output-skill`, plus 3 image-generation-only skills (`imagegen-frontend-web`, `imagegen-frontend-mobile`, `brandkit`). Each skill's job: read a design brief, infer the intended aesthetic, and generate layout/typography/motion (GSAP) code that avoids generic "AI slop" UI patterns — no runtime code, purely instructional SKILL.md content.

Since the task asked for "the skill" (singular) and the repo README explicitly recommends one as the safe default ("Start with **taste-skill** for the safest general default"), and its folder name matches the repo name, I installed only that one — `skills/taste-skill/` (a single 88KB `SKILL.md`, no scripts/references/assets subfolder).

**Installed at:** `C:\Users\Jony.li\.claude\skills\design-taste-frontend\SKILL.md`

I named the folder `design-taste-frontend` (not `taste-skill`) because that's the `name:` field in the SKILL.md frontmatter — the README calls this the skill's "install name," and it matches this machine's existing convention where every installed skill's folder name equals its frontmatter `name:` (verified against `o1key-imagegen` and `comfyui-node-basics`). The skill is now live and shows up in the active skills list.

**Frontmatter:**
```
name: design-taste-frontend
description: Anti-slop frontend skill for landing pages, portfolios, and redesigns. The agent reads the brief, infers the right design direction, and ships interfaces that do not look templated. Real design systems when applicable, audit-first on redesigns, strict pre-flight check.
```

**Extra setup:** None required. It's pure markdown guidance — no dependencies, no API keys, no env vars. (It references GSAP as an animation library within generated code examples, but that's content for whatever project the skill is applied to, not an install-time dependency.)

**Nothing failed or looked suspicious.** Repo is clean MIT-licensed content; only notable item is a standard disclaimer that the project has no official crypto token (unrelated to functionality). 两个子 agent 都完成了，全部成功 ✅。而且 `design-taste-frontend` 技能已经在我当前会话里生效了（系统已加载）。

**1. taste-skill 安装 —— 完成**

有个值得知道的发现：那个仓库其实不是单个技能，而是一个包含 **13 个前端设计类技能**的合集（都是"反 AI 味"的设计指导型技能，纯 SKILL.md 指令，无需任何依赖或 API key）。子 agent 按照仓库 README 的建议（"从 taste-skill 开始，最安全的通用默认"）只安装了旗舰款：

- 安装位置：`C:\Users\Jony.li\.claude\skills\design-taste-frontend\`（文件夹名取自 SKILL.md 里的 `name:` 字段，与你机器上现有技能的命名惯例一致）
- 用途：做落地页、作品集、重设计时避免模板化的"AI 味"界面，先审计再动手
- 仓库里还有 12 个没装的（`redesign-skill`、`minimalist-skill`、`brutalist-skill`、3 个图像生成向的等），需要的话说一声，随时补装

**2. 项目启动依赖 —— 部署完成，已实测能跑**

- 环境：node v24.12.0 / npm 11.6.2，满足 Next.js 15 要求
- `npm install` 干净利落：54 个包，零报错零警告
- 冒烟测试：`npm run dev` 3.3 秒就绪，首页编译后 **HTTP 200**，日志无运行时错误；测完后子 agent 只杀掉了 3000 端口那一个进程，已确认端口释放
- 启动命令：**`npm run dev`** → 打开 http://localhost:3000

**你还差一步才能真正出图**：首次打开页面时右上角 ⚙ 设置面板会自动弹出，需要填入你的 **o1key API Bearer token**（只存本地 `data/settings.json`，已 gitignore）。默认线路是"全球加速 (api.o1key.cn)"，不通可切 CF 加速或美国直连，面板里有"测试连接"按钮。按 HANDOFF.md 记录，链路已用假 token 验证过（返回预期的 401），但真实出图还没用真 token 跑过——填上 token 后可以让我再做一次端到端验证。

两个 worker 合计约 7.5 万 token、并行跑了约 5 分钟。

content.text:
Installation complete and verified. Here is the summary.

## What the skill does

`web-access` gives an agent a full internet-access toolkit: automatic tool selection between WebSearch/WebFetch/curl/Jina and a **CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol) browser proxy**. The CDP proxy connects directly to your actual, already-running Chrome/Edge/Chromium (via each browser's built-in remote-debugging toggle), so it inherits your real login sessions — no separate automation browser, no Playwright/Puppeteer.

Key usage (exact invocations you'll use):
- Pre-flight check (run once per session; auto-starts the proxy): `node "${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/check-deps.mjs"` — optionally `--browser chrome|edge` for a one-off override.
- Once the proxy is up, everything is plain `curl` against `http://localhost:3456`:
  - `curl -s http://localhost:3456/targets` — list open tabs
  - `curl -s -X POST --data-raw 'https://example.com' http://localhost:3456/new` — open a new background tab (URL goes in POST body, not query string, since v2.5.3)
  - `curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:3456/eval?target=ID" -d 'document.title'` — run JS / read DOM
  - `curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:3456/click?target=ID" -d 'button.submit'` — JS click; `/clickAt` for a real mouse event; `/setFiles` for file upload
  - `curl -s "http://localhost:3456/screenshot?target=ID&file=/tmp/shot.png"`, `/scroll`, `/back`, `/close`
- Local bookmark/history lookup: `node "${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/find-url.mjs" <keywords...> [--only bookmarks|history] [--since 7d] [--sort visits]`
- Full HTTP API reference: `references/cdp-api.md`; per-site learned quirks accumulate under `references/site-patterns/<domain>.md`.

Before doing CDP work, the SKILL.md requires the agent to show the user a one-line risk notice about possible automation-detection/ban risk on some sites — this is transparent, user-facing text, not a hidden instruction.

## Install path

`C:\Users\Jony.li\.claude\skills\web-access\` (contains `SKILL.md`, `README.md`, `scripts/`, `references/`, `templates/`, `.claude-plugin/`; no `.git`; verified via `diff -rq` against the fresh clone — identical).

## Frontmatter

```
name: web-access
license: MIT
github: https://github.com/eze-is/web-access
description: 所有联网操作必须通过此 skill 处理，包括：搜索、网页抓取、登录后操作、网络交互等。触发场景：用户要求搜索信息、查看网页内容、访问需要登录的网站、操作网页界面、抓取社交媒体内容（小红书、微博、推特等）、读取动态渲染页面、以及任何需要真实浏览器环境的网络任务。
metadata: author: 一泽Eze, version: "2.5.3"
```

## Runtime dependencies

- **Node.js 22+**: already satisfied (v24.12.0 on this machine). All scripts use only Node built-ins (native WebSocket, http, fs) — there is no `package.json` and nothing to `npm install`. No Playwright/Puppeteer, no MCP server, no heavy download at all — the whole install was a few hundred KB of text/JS.
- **sqlite3 CLI** (only needed for `find-url.mjs`'s browser-history search, not for bookmarks or any CDP/browser-control feature): was missing, so I installed it via `winget install --id SQLite.SQLite -e --source winget` (deliberately targeted the `winget` source, not `msstore`, to avoid a Microsoft Store data-sharing agreement prompt). Verified working: `sqlite3.exe --version` → `3.53.3`. Caveat: winget updated the user PATH, but the currently-running shell/session has a stale PATH snapshot, so `sqlite3` won't resolve by bare name until you restart your terminal / Claude Code session. Until then it's reachable at `C:\Users\Jony.li\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WinGet\Links\sqlite3.exe`.
- Sanity checks run: `node scripts/check-deps.mjs` executed correctly end-to-end (created `config.env` from template, confirmed Node version, correctly detected no browser had remote debugging enabled yet and printed the exact user-facing instructions — this is expected since remote debugging isn't on by default). `node scripts/find-url.mjs --help` printed correct usage.
- Not yet configured (requires your action, one-time): enable remote debugging in whichever browser you want the agent to control — `chrome://inspect/#remote-debugging` or `edge://inspect/#remote-debugging`, check "Allow remote debugging for this browser instance".

## Security review — nothing malicious found

I read `SKILL.md` in full plus every script (`cdp-proxy.mjs`, `check-deps.mjs`, `find-url.mjs`, `browser-discovery.mjs`, `match-site.mjs`) and both plugin manifests before installing. No prompt-injection, no data-exfiltration instructions, no "ignore safety" language. Findings worth flagging for your awareness (disclosed behavior, not hidden, but relevant since it'll sit in agent context):

1. **It can read your full local Chrome/Edge browsing history and bookmarks** (`find-url.mjs` copies the SQLite `History` DB to temp, queries it, then deletes the copy). This only prints to stdout for the agent to use — I found no network exfiltration path in the code — but it's a genuinely powerful, privacy-sensitive capability worth being aware you're granting.
2. **The CDP proxy actively blocks pages from probing the browser's own debug port** (`Fetch.requestPaused` → `failRequest`), i.e., it has built-in anti-detection so sites can't easily tell they're being automated. This is disclosed as intentional "反风控" (anti-risk-control) behavior, consistent with the README's own ban-risk warning, but it is the kind of technique also used by malicious tooling, so I'm flagging it explicitly rather than glossing over it.
3. Optional **Jina** integration (`r.jina.ai/<url>`) routes fetched page content through a third-party service to save tokens — the skill only does this when the agent chooses to invoke it, and it's clearly documented, not silent.

None of this crossed into "malicious" territory (no credential harvesting to a remote server, no attempts to override safety behavior), so I proceeded with installation as instructed. Given points 1 and 2 above, you may want to keep this scoped to a browser profile/account you're comfortable an agent having deep access to.

One environment oddity during install: the first copy attempt into `.claude/skills/web-access/` appeared to succeed, then the directory was reported missing on the very next command, and a later `rm -rf .git` silently no-op'd once. Both were transient — a second attempt succeeded and was verified stable across multiple independent follow-up calls (byte-for-byte `diff` against the clone, repeated `ls`), and Windows Defender showed no threat detections. Worth a quick manual glance at `C:\Users\Jony.li\.claude\skills\web-access\` on your end just to be safe, but current state looks solid.

- Sanity checks run: `node scripts/check-deps.mjs` executed correctly end-to-end (created `config.env` from template, confirmed Node version, correctly detected no browser had remote debugging enabled yet and printed the exact user-facing instructions — this is expected since remote debugging isn't on by default). `node scripts/find-url.mjs --help` printed correct usage.
- Not yet configured (requires your action, one-time): enable remote debugging in whichever browser you want the agent to control — `chrome://inspect/#remote-debugging` or `edge://inspect/#remote-debugging`, check "Allow remote debugging for this browser instance".

## Security review — nothing malicious found

I read `SKILL.md` in full plus every script (`cdp-proxy.mjs`, `check-deps.mjs`, `find-url.mjs`, `browser-discovery.mjs`, `match-site.mjs`) and both plugin manifests before installing. No prompt-injection, no data-exfiltration instructions, no "ignore safety" language. Findings worth flagging for your awareness (disclosed behavior, not hidden, but relevant since it'll sit in agent context):

1. **It can read your full local Chrome/Edge browsing history and bookmarks** (`find-url.mjs` copies the SQLite `History` DB to temp, queries it, then deletes the copy). This only prints to stdout for the agent to use — I found no network exfiltration path in the code — but it's a genuinely powerful, privacy-sensitive capability worth being aware you're granting.
2. **The CDP proxy actively blocks pages from probing the browser's own debug port** (`Fetch.requestPaused` → `failRequest`), i.e., it has built-in anti-detection so sites can't easily tell they're being automated. This is disclosed as intentional "反风控" (anti-risk-control) behavior, consistent with the README's own ban-risk warning, but it is the kind of technique also used by malicious tooling, so I'm flagging it explicitly rather than glossing over it.
3. Optional **Jina** integration (`r.jina.ai/<url>`) routes fetched page content through a third-party service to save tokens — the skill only does this when the agent chooses to invoke it, and it's clearly documented, not silent.

None of this crossed into "malicious" territory (no credential harvesting to a remote server, no attempts to override safety behavior), so I proceeded with installation as instructed. Given points 1 and 2 above, you may want to keep this scoped to a browser profile/account you're comfortable an agent having deep access to.

One environment oddity during install: the first copy attempt into `.claude/skills/web-access/` appeared to succeed, then the directory was reported missing on the very next command, and a later `rm -rf .git` silently no-op'd once. Both were transient — a second attempt succeeded and was verified stable across multiple independent follow-up calls (byte-for-byte `diff` against the clone, repeated `ls`), and Windows Defender showed no threat detections. Worth a quick manual glance at `C:\Users\Jony.li\.claude\skills\web-access\` on your end just to be safe, but current state looks solid.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: C:\Users\Jony.li\.claude\skills\web-access

# web-access Skill

## 前置检查

在开始联网操作前，先检查 CDP 模式可用性：

```bash
node "C:/Users/Jony.li/.claude/skills/web-access/scripts/check-deps.mjs"
```

**Node.js 22+** 必需（使用原生 WebSocket）。

按脚本输出处理：
- `exit 0` → 继续
- `exit 2` → 需询问用户偏好，写入 `C:/Users/Jony.li/.claude/skills/web-access/config.env` 的 `WEB_ACCESS_BROWSER`
- `exit 1` → 按 stdout 错误信息处理。若提示包含「Agent 处理顺序」，按其步骤执行（如先用系统命令打开浏览器后重跑），自动可解则不打扰用户；仍失败再向用户求助

支持参数 `--browser <chrome|edge>` 表达本次临时覆盖（不写 config.env）。

切换浏览器时，proxy 是长驻进程，需先 `pkill -f cdp-proxy.mjs` 再重跑 check-deps。

检查通过后并必须在回复中向用户直接展示以下须知，再启动 CDP Proxy 执行操作：

```
温馨提示：部分站点对浏览器自动化操作检测严格，存在账号封禁风险。已内置防护措施但无法完全避免，Agent 继续操作即视为接受。
```

## 浏览哲学

**像人一样思考，兼顾高效与适应性的完成任务。**

执行任务时不会过度依赖固有印象所规划的步骤，而是带着目标进入，边看边判断，遇到阻碍就解决，发现内容不够就深入——全程围绕「我要达成什么」做决策。这个 skill 的所有行为都应遵循这个逻辑。

**① 拿到请求** — 先明确用户要做什么，定义成功标准：什么算完成了？需要获取什么信息、执行什么操作、达到什么结果？这是后续所有判断的锚点。

**② 选择起点** — 根据任务性质、平台特征、达成条件，选一个最可能直达的方式作为第一步去验证。一次成功当然最好；不成功则在③中调整。比如，需要操作页面、需要登录态、已知静态方式不可达的平台（小红书、微信公众号等）→ 直接 CDP

**③ 过程校验** — 每一步的结果都是证据，不只是成功或失败的二元信号。用结果对照①的成功标准，更新你对目标的判断：路径在推进吗？结果的整体面貌（质量、相关度、量级）是否指向目标可达？发现方向错了立即调整，不在同一个方式上反复重试——搜索没命中不等于"还没找对方法"，也可能是"目标不存在"；API 报错、页面缺少预期元素、重试无改善，都是在告诉你该重新评估方向。遇到弹窗、登录墙等障碍，判断它是否真的挡住了目标：挡住了就处理，没挡住就绕过——内容可能已在页面 DOM 中，交互只是展示手段。

**④ 完成判断** — 对照定义的任务成功标准，确认任务完成后才停止，但也不要过度操作，不为了"完整"而浪费代价。

## 联网工具选择

- **确保信息的真实性，一手信息优于二手信息**：搜索引擎和聚合平台是信息发现入口。当多次搜索尝试后没有质的改进时，升级到更根本的获取方式：定位一手来源（官网、官方平台、原始页面）。

| 场景 | 工具 |
|------|------|
| 搜索摘要或关键词结果，发现信息来源 | **WebSearch** |
| URL 已知，需要从页面定向提取特定信息 | **WebFetch**（拉取网页内容，由小模型根据 prompt 提取，返回处理后结果） |
| URL 已知，需要原始 HTML 源码（meta、JSON-LD 等结构化字段） | **curl** |
| 非公开内容，或已知静态层无效的平台（小红书、微信公众号等公开内容也被反爬限制） | **浏览器 CDP**（直接，跳过静态层） |
| 需要登录态、交互操作，或需要像人一样在浏览器内自由导航探索 | **浏览器 CDP** |

浏览器 CDP 不要求 URL 已知——可从任意入口出发，通过页面内搜索、点击、跳转等方式找到目标内容。WebSearch、WebFetch、curl 均不处理登录态。

**Jina**（可选预处理层，可与 WebFetch/curl 组合使用，由于其特性可节省 tokens 消耗，请积极在任务合适时组合使用）：第三方网络服务，可将网页转为 Markdown，大幅节省 token 但可能有信息损耗。调用方式为 `r.jina.ai/example.com`（URL 前加前缀，不保留原网址 http 前缀），限 20 RPM。适合文章、博客、文档、PDF 等以正文为核心的页面；对数据面板、商品页等非文章结构页面可能提取到错误区块。

进入浏览器层后，`/eval` 就是你的眼睛和手：

- **看**：用 `/eval` 查询 DOM，发现页面上的链接、按钮、表单、文本内容——相当于「看看这个页面有什么」
- **做**：用 `/click` 点击元素、`/scroll` 滚动加载、`/eval` 填表提交——像人一样在页面内自然导航
- **读**：用 `/eval` 提取文字内容，判断图片/视频是否承载核心信息——是则提取媒体 URL 定向读取或 `/screenshot` 视觉识别

浏览网页时，**先了解页面结构，再决定下一步动作**。不需要提前规划所有步骤。

### 补充：本地浏览器资源

用户指向**本人访问过的页面**（"我之前看的那个讲 X 的文章"、"上次打开过的 XX 面板"）或**组织内部系统**（"我们的 XX 平台"、"公司那个 YY 系统"等公网搜不到的目标）时，检索本地浏览器（Chrome / Edge）书签/历史：

```bash
node "C:/Users/Jony.li/.claude/skills/web-access/scripts/find-url.mjs" [关键词...] [--only bookmarks|history] [--browser chrome|edge] [--limit N] [--since 1d|7h|YYYY-MM-DD] [--sort recent|visits]
```

关键词空格分词、多词 AND，匹配 title + url（可省略）；默认遍历所有已安装的 Chromium 系浏览器（Chrome、Edge），`--browser` 限定单一来源；`--since` / `--sort` 仅作用于历史；默认按最近访问倒序，`--sort visits` 按访问次数排序（适合"高频访问的网站"这类场景）。

### 程序化操作与 GUI 交互

浏览器内操作页面有两种方式：

- **程序化方式**（构造 URL 直接导航、eval 操作 DOM）：成功时速度快、精确，但对网站来说不是正常用户行为，可能触发反爬机制。
- **GUI 交互**（点击按钮、填写输入框、滚动浏览）：GUI 是为人设计的，网站不会限制正常的 UI 操作，确定性最高，但步骤多、速度慢。

根据对目标平台的了解来灵活选择方式。GUI 交互也是程序化方式的有效探测——通过一次真实交互观察站点的实际行为（URL 模式、必需参数、页面跳转逻辑），为后续程序化操作提供依据；同时当程序化方式受阻时，GUI 交互是可靠的兜底。

**站点内交互产生的链接是可靠的**：通过用户视角中的可交互单元（卡片、条目、按钮）进行的站点内交互，自然到达的 URL 天然携带平台所需的完整上下文。而手动构造的 URL 可能缺失隐式必要参数，导致被拦截、返回错误页面、甚至触发反爬。

## 浏览器 CDP 模式

通过 CDP Proxy 直连用户日常浏览器（Chrome / Edge / Chromium 等 Chromium 系），天然携带登录态，无需启动独立浏览器。
若无用户明确要求，不主动操作用户已有 tab，所有操作都在自己创建的后台 tab 中进行，保持对用户环境的最小侵入。不关闭用户 tab 的前提下，完成任务后关闭自己创建的 tab，保持环境整洁。

### 启动

```bash
node "C:/Users/Jony.li/.claude/skills/web-access/scripts/check-deps.mjs"
```

脚本会依次检查 Node.js、浏览器调试端口，并确保 Proxy 已连接（未运行则自动启动并等待）。Proxy 启动后持续运行。

### Proxy API

所有操作通过 curl 调用 HTTP API：

```bash
# 列出用户已打开的 tab
curl -s http://localhost:3456/targets

# 创建新后台 tab（自动等待加载）— URL 走 POST body，避免目标 URL 含 query 时被切分
curl -s -X POST --data-raw 'https://example.com' http://localhost:3456/new

# 页面信息
curl -s "http://localhost:3456/info?target=ID"

# 执行任意 JS：可读写 DOM、提取数据、操控元素、触发状态变更、提交表单、调用内部方法
curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:3456/eval?target=ID" -d 'document.title'

# 捕获页面渲染状态（含视频当前帧）
curl -s "http://localhost:3456/screenshot?target=ID&file=/tmp/shot.png"

# 导航（URL 走 POST body，target 走 query）、后退
curl -s -X POST --data-raw 'https://example.com' "http://localhost:3456/navigate?target=ID"
curl -s "http://localhost:3456/back?target=ID"

# 点击（POST body 为 CSS 选择器）— JS el.click()，简单快速，覆盖大多数场景
curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:3456/click?target=ID" -d 'button.submit'

# 真实鼠标点击 — CDP Input.dispatchMouseEvent，算用户手势，能触发文件对话框
curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:3456/clickAt?target=ID" -d 'button.upload'

# 文件上传 — 直接设置 file input 的本地文件路径，绕过文件对话框
curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:3456/setFiles?target=ID" -d '{"selector":"input[type=file]","files":["/path/to/file.png"]}'

# 滚动（触发懒加载）
curl -s "http://localhost:3456/scroll?target=ID&y=3000"
curl -s "http://localhost:3456/scroll?target=ID&direction=bottom"

# 关闭 tab
curl -s "http://localhost:3456/close?target=ID"
```

### 页面内导航

两种方式打开页面内的链接：

- **`/click`**：在当前 tab 内直接点击用户视角中的可交互单元，简单直接，串行处理。适合需要在同一页面内连续操作的场景，如点击展开、翻页、进入详情等。
- **`/new` + 完整 URL**：使用目标链接的完整地址（包含所有URL参数），在新 tab 中打开。适合需要同时访问多个页面的场景。

很多网站的链接包含会话相关的参数（如 token），这些参数是正常访问所必需的。提取 URL 时应保留完整地址，不要裁剪或省略参数。URL 通过 POST body 原样传入 `/new` 或 `/navigate`。

> **v2.5.3 迁移提示**：若引用的站点经验文件（`references/site-patterns/*.md`）或其它脚本中仍含 `GET /new?url=...` 或 `/navigate?target=...&url=...` 的旧写法，调用会收到迁移指引。按 [`references/migration-2.5.3.md`](references/migration-2.5.3.md) 就地改写为 POST body 后再使用，并顺手把该站点经验文件更新掉。

### 媒体资源提取

判断内容在图片里时，用 `/eval` 从 DOM 直接拿图片 URL，再定向读取——比全页截图精准得多。

### 技术事实
- 页面中存在大量已加载但未展示的内容——轮播中非当前帧的图片、折叠区块的文字、懒加载占位元素等，它们存在于 DOM 中但对用户不可见。以数据结构（容器、属性、节点关系）为单位思考，可以直接触达这些内容。
- DOM 中存在选择器不可跨越的边界（Shadow DOM 的 `shadowRoot`、iframe 的 `contentDocument`等）。eval 递归遍历可一次穿透所有层级，返回带标签的结构化内容，适合快速了解未知页面的完整结构。
- `/scroll` 到底部会触发懒加载，使未进入视口的图片完成加载。提取图片 URL 前若未滚动，部分图片可能尚未加载。
- 拿到媒体资源 URL 后，公开资源可直接下载到本地后用读取；需要登录态才可获取的资源才需要在浏览器内 navigate + screenshot。
- 短时间内密集打开大量页面（如批量 `/new`）可能触发网站的反爬风控。
- 平台返回的"内容不存在""页面不见了"等提示不一定反映真实状态，也可能是访问方式的问题（如 URL 缺失必要参数、触发反爬）而非内容本身的问题。

### 视频内容获取

用户浏览器真实渲染，截图可捕获当前视频帧。核心能力：通过 `/eval` 操控 `<video>` 元素（获取时长、seek 到任意时间点、播放/暂停/全屏），配合 `/screenshot` 采帧，可对视频内容进行离散采样分析。

### 登录判断

用户日常浏览器天然携带登录态，大多数常用网站已登录。

登录判断的核心问题只有一个：**目标内容拿到了吗？**

打开页面后先尝试获取目标内容。只有当确认**目标内容无法获取**且判断登录能解决时，才告知用户：
> "当前页面在未登录状态下无法获取[具体内容]，请在你的浏览器中登录 [网站名]，完成后告诉我继续。"

登录完成后无需重启任何东西，直接刷新页面继续。

### 任务结束

用 `/close` 关闭自己创建的 tab，必须保留用户原有的 tab 不受影响。

Proxy 持续运行，不建议主动停止——重启后需要在浏览器中重新授权 CDP 连接。

## 并行调研：子 Agent 分治策略

任务包含多个**独立**调研目标时（如同时调研 N 个项目、N 个来源），鼓励合理分治给子 Agent 并行执行，而非主 Agent 串行处理。

**好处：**
- **速度**：多子 Agent 并行，总耗时约等于单个子任务时长
- **上下文保护**：抓取内容不进入主 Agent 上下文，主 Agent 只接收摘要，节省 token

**并行 CDP 操作**：每个子 Agent 在当前用户浏览器实例中，自行创建所需的后台 tab（`/new`），自行操作，任务结束自行关闭（`/close`）。所有子 Agent 共享一个浏览器、一个 Proxy，通过不同 targetId 操作不同 tab，无竞态风险。

**子 Agent Prompt 写法：目标导向，而非步骤指令**
- 必须在子 Agent prompt 中写 `必须加载 web-access skill 并遵循指引` ，子 Agent 会自动加载 skill，无需在 prompt 中复制 skill 内容或指定路径。
- 子 Agent 有自主判断能力。主 Agent 的职责是说清楚**要什么**，仅在必要与确信时限定**怎么做**。过度指定步骤会剥夺子 Agent 的判断空间，反而引入主 Agent 的假设错误。**避免 prompt 用词对子 Agent 行为的暗示**：「搜索xx」会把子 Agent 锚定到 WebSearch，而实际上有些反爬站点需要 CDP 直接访问主站才能有效获取内容。主 Agent 写 prompt 时应描述目标（「获取」「调研」「了解」），避免用暗示具体手段的动词（「搜索」「抓取」「爬取」）。

**分治判断标准：**

| 适合分治 | 不适合分治 |
|----------|-----------|
| 目标相互独立，结果互不依赖 | 目标有依赖关系，下一个需要上一个的结果 |
| 每个子任务量足够大（多页抓取、多轮搜索） | 简单单页查询，分治开销大于收益 |
| 需要 CDP 浏览器或长时间运行的任务 | 几次 WebSearch / Jina 就能完成的轻量查询 |

## 信息核实类任务

核实的目标是**一手来源**，而非更多的二手报道。多个媒体引用同一个错误会造成循环印证假象。

搜索引擎和聚合平台是信息发现入口，是**定位**信息的工具，不可用于直接**证明**真伪。找到来源后，直接访问读取原文。同一原则适用于工具能力/用法的调研——官方文档是一手来源，不确定时先查文档或源码，不猜测。

| 信息类型 | 一手来源 |
|----------|---------|
| 政策/法规 | 发布机构官网 |
| 企业公告 | 公司官方新闻页 |
| 学术声明 | 原始论文/机构官网 |
| 工具能力/用法 | 官方文档、源码 |

**找不到官网时**：权威媒体的原创报道（非转载）可作为次级依据，但需向用户说明："未找到官方原文，以下核实来自[媒体名]报道，存在转述误差可能。"单一来源时同样向用户声明。

## 站点经验

操作中积累的特定网站经验，按域名存储在 `references/site-patterns/` 下。

确定目标网站后，如果前置检查输出的 site-patterns 列表中有匹配的站点，必须读取对应文件获取先验知识（平台特征、有效模式、已知陷阱）。经验内容标注了发现日期，当作可能有效的提示而非保证——如果按经验操作失败，回退通用模式并更新经验文件。

CDP 操作成功完成后，如果发现了有必要记录经验的新站点或新模式（URL 结构、平台特征、操作策略），主动写入对应的站点经验文件。只写经过验证的事实，不写未确认的猜测。

文件格式：
```markdown
---
domain: example.com
aliases: [示例, Example]
updated: 2026-03-19
---
## 平台特征
架构、反爬行为、登录需求、内容加载方式等事实

## 有效模式
已验证的 URL 模式、操作策略、选择器

## 已知陷阱
什么会失败以及为什么
```
经验/陷阱内容标注发现日期，当作"可能有效的提示"而非"保证正确的事实"。

## References 索引

| 文件 | 何时加载 |
|------|---------|
| `references/cdp-api.md` | 需要 CDP API 详细参考、JS 提取模式、错误处理时 |
| `references/site-patterns/{domain}.md` | 确定目标网站后，读取对应站点经验 |

There's a message about a potential authorization popup in the browser, though that's typically only needed for the edge://inspect flow — with a manual debugging port and custom user-data-dir, CDP should be directly accessible without that step. The proxy tried connecting to the WebSocket on localhost:9222 and failed. The shell's proxy environment variables (autoproxy 127.0.0.1:10808) might be routing localhost through the proxy — Node's WebSocket implementation could be inheriting those env vars, though undici-based WebSocket doesn't typically respect HTTP_PROXY by default. Another possibility: Edge 150 might have extended the authorization requirement even with a custom user-data-dir, or there's an authorization popup visible in the Edge window that's blocking the connection. I should check the proxy log file and see if the proxy process is actually running, and look at the Edge window to see if there's a popup waiting for user interaction.

The most likely fix is to clear the proxy environment variables when running check-deps — setting NO_PROXY=localhost,127.0.0.1 or unsetting http_proxy/https_proxy/all_proxy entirely, then retrying with the log to see what's actually happening.

content.thinking:
So the proxy is running on port 3456 and responding with a connection error, but it can't reach the browser's debugging port at 9222. The issue is that cdp-proxy inherited the shell's HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY environment variables (pointing to 127.0.0.1:10808), which is causing its WebSocket client to route through that proxy instead of connecting directly to the browser.

Node's native fetch (Undici) doesn't honor proxy environment variables by default unless NODE_USE_ENV_PROXY is explicitly set, so that might not be the culprit. But there's another strong possibility: an IPv6/IPv4 mismatch. Edge is binding to 127.0.0.1:9222 (IPv4 only), but Node might be resolving "localhost" to ::1 (IPv6) first, which would get refused. This is a known issue on Windows with Node 17+ where DNS resolution changed to "verbatim" mode. curl worked because it tries both addresses and prefers IPv4, but the CDP client is hitting the IPv6 address and failing.

Interestingly, check-deps reported that the browser check passed on port 9222, so something about how it's connecting is different from what cdp-proxy is doing.

Let me try a direct approach: fetch the /json/version endpoint to get the actual webSocketDebuggerUrl that Edge is returning, then attempt a WebSocket connection from Node to that exact URL to see where it fails. The real issue is likely IPv6 — Edge binds to 127.0.0.1 only, but Node 24's resolver returns ::1 first. Let me test this empirically by fetching the WebSocket URL and trying connections to both localhost and 127.0.0.1 variants with a timeout, then grep the proxy and discovery scripts to see how they construct and discover the WebSocket endpoint.

name: orchestrator-sonnet-workers
description: 用户要求主会话（Fable 5）当大脑做任务拆解与管理，具体执行派发给跑 claude-sonnet-5 的子 agent
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: feedback
  originSessionId: cf8c0b97-09bd-45d1-958f-d6f2e0428651
---

用户（2026-07-08）明确要求的协作模式：主会话作为编排者/大脑，负责理解需求、拆分任务、汇总验证；具体执行工作交给子 agent，且子 agent 必须使用 `claude-sonnet-5`。

**Why:** 用户走的是 nexaxis.ai 自定义网关，希望把贵的 Fable 5 只用在规划/决策上，批量执行用 Sonnet 5 控制成本。

**How to apply:** 已在 `~/.claude/settings.json` 的 `env` 里设置 `CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL: "claude-sonnet-5"`，它强制所有子 agent（含 Workflow 里的 agent()）跑 Sonnet 5，优先级高于 per-call `model` 参数和 agent 定义的 frontmatter。因此正常用 Agent 工具派发即可，无需每次传 model。对可拆分的任务应主动拆解并行派发，而不是自己在主循环里逐个执行。若某个子任务确实需要 Fable 5，需先提醒用户该环境变量会覆盖 per-call 指定。

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review
- security-review

NETWORK_ROUTES[DEFAULT_ROUTE]; } ``` - `submitTask` 里的网络错误提示 `请在设置里换一条线路（CF加速 / 美国直连），或检查网络。` 改为 `请检查网络。` ## 4. src/lib/settings.ts - `DEFAULTS` 删除 `baseUrlOverride: "",`。 - `readSettings` 需要清洗旧版 data/settings.json（磁盘上可能残留 route: "CF加速"/"美国直连" 和 baseUrlOverride 字段），改为逐字段显式构造，不要整体 spread parsed： ```ts const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as Partial & Record; return { apiKey: typeof parsed.apiKey === "string" ? parsed.apiKey : "", route: DEFAULT_ROUTE, defaults: { ...DEFAULTS.defaults, ...(parsed.defaults || {}) }, }; ``` - `writeSettings` 不用改（Settings 类型收窄后它自然不再写入 baseUrlOverride）。 - ⚠️ 禁止读取/打印 `data/settings.json` 的内容——里面有用户真实 API 密钥。也不要修改这个数据文件，运行时清洗即可。 ## 5. src/app/api/settings/route.ts - 删除 `VALID_ROUTES` 常量、route 校验分支、baseUrlOverride 分支、defaults 分支。POST 只处理 `apiKey`（非空 string 则 trim 存入）和 `clearApiKey === true`。 - patch 的类型注解相应收窄为 `Partial<{ apiKey: string }> & { clearApiKey?: boolean }`。 - 清理不再使用的 import（`Billing, ModelName, Resolution, RouteName, SettingsDefaults` 应该都用不到了）。GET 不动。 ## 6. src/app/api/settings/test/route.ts - 删除 override 相关逻辑（第 19 行）；`route` 直接用存储值：`const s = await readSettings();` 后 `const baseUrl = resolveBaseUrl(s.route);`（body.route/body.baseUrlOverride 都不再读取，body 里只可能带 apiKey——`const apiKey = (typeof body.apiKey === "string" && body.apiKey.trim()) || s.apiKey;` 这行保留）。 - 最后 catch 里的提示 `。可尝试切换线路。` 改为 `。请检查网络。` - 清理不再使用的 `RouteName` import。 ## 7. src/app/api/jobs/route.ts - 第 48 行 `resolveBaseUrl(s.route, s.baseUrlOverride)` → `resolveBaseUrl(s.route)`。 ## 8. src/app/api/jobs/[id]/route.ts - 第 34 行同样改为 `resolveBaseUrl(s.route)`。 ## 9. src/components/SettingsPanel.tsx（改动最大） 最终形态要求： - 删除 `

o1key 接入

`（section 保留，标题去掉）。 - 「API 令牌 (Bearer)」字段原样保留。 - 「线路」字段改为**只读展示行**（不再是 Select，也去掉旧 hint「连接失败时可切换线路重试」）： ```tsx
{ROUTE_OPTIONS[0].label}
``` - 删除「自定义 Base URL（可选）」整个 Field 及 `baseUrl`/`setBaseUrl` state。 - 删除整个「默认生成参数」section（h3 + 四个 Select 网格），以及 `defs`/`setDefs` state、`onModel` 函数、`resOptions` 变量。 - `route` state 删除（不再需要，服务端固定全球加速）。 - useEffect 简化为只重置 `setApiKey(""); setTestMsg(null);`（依赖 `[settings]` 保留）。 - `test()` 的请求体改为：`const body: Record = {}; if (apiKey.trim()) body.apiKey = apiKey.trim();` - `save()` 的请求体同样只带 apiKey（有值时）；删除 `updateParams({...})` 调用及 `updateParams` 的 store 取值。 - 「测试连接」按钮、testMsg 展示、底部隐私说明段落、「保存设置」按钮全部保留。 - 清理 import：`ASPECT_RATIOS, BILLINGS, MODELS, resolutionsFor` 不再需要（只留 `ROUTE_OPTIONS`）；`Select` 不再需要（`Button, Field` 保留）;类型 import 里 `Billing, ModelName, Resolution, RouteName, SettingsDefaults` 都不再需要（如果一个都不用就删掉整行 type import）。`motion`、`cn`、`Icon`、`useStudio` 保留。 ## 10. src/components/GenerateBar.tsx - 第 89 行 toast 文案 `提交失败，请检查网络或线路` → `提交失败，请检查网络`。 ## 11. 文档（轻量） - 在 README.md 和 HANDOFF.md 里 grep `CF加速|美国直连|自定义 Base URL|baseUrlOverride|默认生成参数`，把提到多线路切换/自定义 URL/设置面板默认参数区块的句子最小幅度改成新现实（只有全球加速一条线路、设置面板只管 API 令牌）。不要重写文档，只微调相关句子。 # 验证（必须做） 1. `cd "C:/Users/Jony.li/Desktop/o1key-vision-workbench" && npx tsc --noEmit` → 必须 0 错误。 2. `npm run build` → 必须成功。若失败，修复到通过为止（修复也要符合上述规格）。 3. `git diff --stat` 确认只改了上述文件。 - 不要 git commit，不要启动 dev server。 # 返回格式 返回：改动文件清单（每个一句话说明）、tsc 与 build 的结果（原样贴关键输出行）、以及你对规格外做的任何额外修复（如有）。

content.text:
This matches the spec exactly. All edits are complete and verified.

## Summary

**Files changed (12, matching git diff --stat exactly):**

1. `src/lib/types.ts` — `RouteName` narrowed to `"全球加速"`; removed `baseUrlOverride` from `Settings`.
2. `src/lib/models.ts` — `ROUTE_OPTIONS` now has only the 全球加速 entry.
3. `src/lib/o1key.ts` — `NETWORK_ROUTES` reduced to one entry; `resolveBaseUrl` dropped its `override` param; `submitTask`'s network-error message shortened to "...请检查网络。".
4. `src/lib/settings.ts` — `DEFAULTS` drops `baseUrlOverride`; `readSettings` now explicitly constructs each field (forces `route` to `DEFAULT_ROUTE`, only trusts `apiKey`/`defaults` from disk) instead of spreading the parsed JSON, so legacy `route: "CF加速"/"美国直连"` and stale `baseUrlOverride` on disk are scrubbed at read time. Did not read/print `data/settings.json` contents at any point.
5. `src/app/api/settings/route.ts` — removed `VALID_ROUTES`, route/baseUrlOverride/defaults handling; POST now only accepts `apiKey` and `clearApiKey`; unused type imports dropped.
6. `src/app/api/settings/test/route.ts` — removed override logic; `baseUrl` now derives from `resolveBaseUrl(s.route)` only; catch message changed to "...请检查网络。"; removed unused `RouteName` import.
7. `src/app/api/jobs/route.ts` — `resolveBaseUrl(s.route, s.baseUrlOverride)` → `resolveBaseUrl(s.route)`.
8. `src/app/api/jobs/[id]/route.ts` — same one-line change.
9. `src/components/SettingsPanel.tsx` — removed the "o1key 接入" `h3`; 线路 field is now a read-only display row using `ROUTE_OPTIONS[0].label`; deleted the custom Base URL field and the entire "默认生成参数" section along with `route`/`baseUrl`/`defs` state, `onModel`, `resOptions`, and the `updateParams` call; `test()`/`save()` request bodies now only send `apiKey` when present; trimmed imports to `ROUTE_OPTIONS`, `Button`, `Field` (no more `Select`, no more unused type imports).
10. `src/components/GenerateBar.tsx` — toast text "提交失败，请检查网络或线路" → "提交失败，请检查网络".
11. `README.md` / `HANDOFF.md` — updated the 线路/默认参数 bullets and the troubleshooting line to reflect a single fixed 全球加速 route and a settings panel that only manages the API token (also trimmed the now-inaccurate "默认参数" README bullet describing settings-panel default editing, since that capability no longer exists there — it wasn't hit by the literal grep pattern but describes the exact removed feature).

**Verification:**
- `npx tsc --noEmit` → no output, 0 errors.
- `npm run build` → `✓ Compiled successfully in 18.8s`, `✓ Generating static pages (4/4)`, full route manifest printed, no errors (only a pre-existing, unrelated workspace-root/lockfile warning from Next.js).
- `git diff --stat` → exactly the 12 files listed above, nothing else.

**Extra fix beyond spec:** `npx tsc --noEmit` incidentally generated a stray untracked `tsconfig.tsbuildinfo` build artifact in the repo root; I deleted it so the working tree only contains the intended source/doc changes. No other out-of-spec changes were made; `settings.defaults` model, `Studio.tsx`, and the `writeSettings` function were left untouched as instructed.

5. `src/app/api/settings/route.ts` — removed `VALID_ROUTES`, route/baseUrlOverride/defaults handling; POST now only accepts `apiKey` and `clearApiKey`; unused type imports dropped.
6. `src/app/api/settings/test/route.ts` — removed override logic; `baseUrl` now derives from `resolveBaseUrl(s.route)` only; catch message changed to "...请检查网络。"; removed unused `RouteName` import.
7. `src/app/api/jobs/route.ts` — `resolveBaseUrl(s.route, s.baseUrlOverride)` → `resolveBaseUrl(s.route)`.
8. `src/app/api/jobs/[id]/route.ts` — same one-line change.
9. `src/components/SettingsPanel.tsx` — removed the "o1key 接入" `h3`; 线路 field is now a read-only display row using `ROUTE_OPTIONS[0].label`; deleted the custom Base URL field and the entire "默认生成参数" section along with `route`/`baseUrl`/`defs` state, `onModel`, `resOptions`, and the `updateParams` call; `test()`/`save()` request bodies now only send `apiKey` when present; trimmed imports to `ROUTE_OPTIONS`, `Button`, `Field` (no more `Select`, no more unused type imports).
10. `src/components/GenerateBar.tsx` — toast text "提交失败，请检查网络或线路" → "提交失败，请检查网络".
11. `README.md` / `HANDOFF.md` — updated the 线路/默认参数 bullets and the troubleshooting line to reflect a single fixed 全球加速 route and a settings panel that only manages the API token (also trimmed the now-inaccurate "默认参数" README bullet describing settings-panel default editing, since that capability no longer exists there — it wasn't hit by the literal grep pattern but describes the exact removed feature).

3. **默认生成参数区块已删** — 设置面板不再有模型/分辨率/计费/比例四个选择器。底层数据模型保留着（生成接口和页面初始化还依赖它的兜底值），只是不再提供编辑入口。
4. **「o1key 接入」标题已去掉** — 现在面板打开就直接是 API 令牌输入框。

设置面板现在只剩：API 令牌输入 + 线路展示 + 测试连接 + 保存按钮，代码净减了 100 多行。

**验证结果**：`tsc --noEmit` 零错误，`npm run build` 成功；刚才还发现 3000 端口挂着一个跑旧代码的孤儿进程，已清掉换成干净的新服务器，实测 `/api/settings` 返回的已是新结构（不含 baseUrlOverride，route 固定全球加速）。

改动尚未 commit，你在页面上确认效果满意后我再提交。另外令牌目前还没设置（`hasApiKey: false`），要试真实生图的话在新面板里填一下即可。

message.content: 这里的测试连接，采用的什么方式。

content.text:
它是一个**零消耗的探测请求**：拿一个故意不存在的任务 ID 去查询任务状态接口，靠返回的 HTTP 状态码判断连通性和令牌有效性，不花任何额度。

具体流程（`src/app/api/settings/test/route.ts`）：

1. 前端点「测试连接」→ POST 到本地 `/api/settings/test`（只带你刚输入的令牌；没输入就用已保存的）。
2. **服务端**（Next.js API 路由，令牌不经过浏览器直连外网）向 `https://api.o1key.cn/async/v1/tasks/connectivity-probe-000` 发一个 GET，带上 `Authorization: Bearer <令牌>`。
3. 按结果分三种情况：
   - **请求发不出去**（超时/DNS 失败等）→ 「无法连接」，说明网络到 o1key 不通；
   - **返回 401/403** → 网络通，但「令牌被拒绝」，说明 key 不对；
   - **返回其他任何状态码**（比如查不到这个假任务的 404）→ 网络通、令牌通过了认证，显示「连接正常」。

代码注释里也写明了设计取舍：不真正发起一次生图就无法 100% 验证令牌的额度是否够用，所以测试连接只验证"通不通 + 令牌是否被认"，**额度校验留到第一次真实生成时**——这也是界面上提示"首次生成时会真正校验额度"的原因。

选这个方式的好处：查询一个不存在的任务不产生计费，比发一次真实生图便宜；而且认证失败（401/403）和业务性的"任务不存在"（404）能清晰区分开。
